summary: Remove the obsolete Q-0001 blocker diagnostic after Unicode-3.0 became an allowed license, and prevent the resolved policy copy from silently returning.
specSections: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates

decisions.decision: Delete the special-case message that called Unicode-3.0 blocked by Q-0001 and guard the license command source against reintroducing that resolved blocker copy.
decisions.rationale: Unicode-3.0 is now deliberately permitted. An unknown required term must still fail through the general unapproved-license path, but the command must not falsely attribute that failure to the allowed Unicode license or an already-resolved question.

testEvidence.criterion: The resolved Q-0001 blocker cannot silently return in license command output
testEvidence.test: A focused policy regression reads the executable license command and rejects the exact obsolete blocker diagnostic while retaining the expression-policy tests.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 1 failed; Tests 1 failed and 4 passed because scripts/check-licenses.mjs still contained the resolved Q-0001 blocker copy.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 1 passed; Tests 5 passed. The stale diagnostic is absent and compound-expression behavior remains covered.

testEvidence.criterion: Exact Rust and combined dependency-license gates
testEvidence.test: Run both owner-requested commands after removing the stale diagnostic.

red.command: The retained Q-0001 baseline policy run in provenance/entries/2026-07-11-q0001-unicode-license.json
red.outcome: BASELINE POLICY RED: Rust and combined modes each rejected the 19 then-unapproved Unicode-3.0 records before the owner resolution was implemented.

green.command: pnpm.cmd check:licenses rust; pnpm.cmd check:licenses all
green.outcome: GREEN: Rust mode passed 492 packages; combined mode passed 119 npm packages and 492 Rust packages. Every command exited 0.

questions: Q-0001 remains resolved by the owner's explicit policy decision; this cleanup changes no allowed-license semantics.
No owner similarity gate, comparison repository, workflow content, or prohibited source was opened or executed.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
